novel_features.name: Cross-network unified search
novel_features.description: One query across OpenTable and Tock simultaneously, ranked by relevance, earliest availability, and price band.
novel_features.rationale: Requires concurrent calls to both networks and a local join across `restaurants` + `availability_slots` to merge and rank. No single API does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a user asks an agent to find a table, this is the single command that searches both reservation networks and returns structured ranked results — agents do not need to know which network covers which restaurant.

novel_features.name: Cross-network cancellation watcher
novel_features.description: Persistent local watcher that polls both networks for openings on your target venues and party size, with notifications and optional auto-book.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local SQLite `watches` table, adaptive polling per source, and a local notifier. The API endpoints are commodity availability calls; the watch itself does not exist on either network.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Resy's Notify covers Resy only; tockstalk covers Tock only; restaurant-mcp's snipe covers Resy+OT only. None covers both networks; none persists state. Use this when an agent or user needs a hot reservation that isn't currently available.

novel_features.name: Multi-venue earliest available
novel_features.description: Across a list of restaurants from either network, return the earliest open slot per venue within a time horizon.
novel_features.rationale: Composes N parallel availability calls across both networks and ranks by minimum slot timestamp. No single endpoint answers 'soonest among these N venues across two networks'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a user gives an agent a shortlist of venues and wants the soonest opportunity, this is the right shape — one structured response with one row per venue across both networks.

novel_features.name: Per-restaurant change feed
novel_features.description: Show what changed at a specific venue since the last sync — new experiences, slot price moves, hours changes.
novel_features.rationale: Snapshot diff across a single venue's `availability_slots`, `experiences`, and restaurant fields between prior and current sync. Pure local-store comparison.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Hot-target deep-watch: when an agent or user is hunting one venue, drift surfaces every meaningful change since the last look.

narrative.headline: One reservation CLI for OpenTable and Tock — search both networks at once, watch for cancellations, book, and track changes from a local store agents can query.
narrative.value_prop: OpenTable and Tock split the US fine-dining world between them and share zero data. This CLI unifies them: `goat` searches both at once, `watch` polls both for cancellations, `earliest` composes availability across both, and `drift` surfaces what changed at a venue since your last look. Auth is one `auth login --chrome` import — your real Chrome cookies for both sites, no partner keys.
narrative.auth_narrative: Two layers. Anonymous reads (search, restaurant detail, availability) work out of the box via Surf with a Chrome TLS fingerprint that clears Akamai (OpenTable) and Cloudflare (Tock). Authenticated commands (`book`, `wishlist`, `reservations list`) need one-time `auth login --chrome` to import your already-logged-in cookies from your local Chrome profile. There are no API keys to manage; the OpenTable Partner API is out of scope.

quickstart.comment: import cookies from your Chrome profile so authenticated commands work
confirm both opentable.com and exploretock.com sessions imported
headline command — single ranked list across both networks
set up a cancellation watch and let the printer poll both networks adaptively
soonest open slot per venue across both networks

troubleshoots.fix: the persisted-query hash drifted; run `table-reservation-goat-pp-cli doctor --refresh-hashes` to bootstrap the current hash from a fresh homepage fetch
Surf transport with Chrome TLS clears this automatically; if you see a 403, run `table-reservation-goat-pp-cli doctor` to verify the Surf fingerprint is loaded
run `table-reservation-goat-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import cookies, then `auth status` to confirm both networks are signed in

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty availability results for a venue you know has openings
troubleshoots.fix: check `--party` and `--time` (Tock returns empty when no slot matches the seating area filter); also try `goat <venue> --debug` to see the per-network response

troubleshoots.symptom: Watch never fires even though slots opened on the website
troubleshoots.fix: verify `watch list --json` shows your watch `state: active` and `last_polled_at` recent; if the limiter is throttled the typed `RateLimitError` will be in the recent log — increase `--cadence` to back off

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI any time a user or agent needs to search, compare, watch, or book across OpenTable and Tock together — and especially for multi-venue questions ('soonest table at any of these'), cancellation hunting, or tracking changes at a specific venue. For single-network simple lookups, the official site UI is faster.

recipes.title: Headline omakase search across both networks (agent-shaped)
recipes.explanation: Single command, ranked merged output with the deeply-nested fields agents actually need — narrows a multi-KB response to five columns.

recipes.title: Watch one Tock-only and one OT-only venue at the same party size
recipes.command: table-reservation-goat-pp-cli watch add 'alinea' --party 2 --window 'sat 7-9pm' --notify local && table-reservation-goat-pp-cli watch add 'le-bernardin' --party 2 --window 'sat 7-9pm' --notify local
recipes.explanation: Two watches, one local store, one polling daemon — the printer handles both networks via per-source adaptive limiters.

recipes.title: Soonest table among my shortlist
recipes.explanation: One row per venue with the soonest slot, sortable by slot time. Agents pipe into a planner without re-querying.

recipes.title: Watched venue: what changed in the last week
recipes.explanation: Snapshot diff at a single venue — new experiences, slot price moves, hours changes — exactly what hot-target hunters need.

recipes.title: Verify the cookie session for both networks before booking
recipes.explanation: Quick health check before authenticated commands. Use this in agent loops as a precondition for `reservations book`.
